_notes: Mirrors the standard default learning config for parametric training.
Best-checkpoint selection monitors the held-out validation val_loss.
This is intended for Colab GPU runtimes and standard CLI training.

Speed: benchmark=true lets cudnn autotune the conv stack (batches are fixed-size now that the parametric trainer groups each batch into one capture); deterministic is intentionally NOT set because it would disable that autotuning and force slower conv kernels.
num_workers is 0 on purpose: the dataset serves in-memory tensor slices, so worker processes add IPC overhead without a real prefetch win. Bump it only if you measure a data-loading stall.

precision is 32-true (full fp32): this model is tiny (channels=8), so fp16 mixed precision gives little speedup and its narrow range overflowed to inf ESR. Stay on fp32 unless the net grows large enough to benefit from Tensor Cores.

gradient_clip_val=1.0 caps the global grad norm so a single noisy capture can't spike the hypernetwork weights into divergence. This is what let batch_size=16 blow up to inf validation ESR mid-run while batch_size=32 stayed stable; clipping makes small batches safe.

Pair the learning rate with the batch size (linear-scaling rule): lr=0.002 for batch_size=16, lr=0.004 for batch_size=32. The 0.004 that was stable at 32 was too hot for the noisier 16-sample gradients.
